title: DORA Information Sharing — Indicator Distribution to Subsidiaries and Branches Coverage

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run, then normalised in Phase A (clause 'unspecified' → 'Art.5'). Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).

app: HTTP Event Collector from SOAR or threat intel platform
spl:
index=threat sourcetype="ti:bundle_push" earliest=-30d
| stats latest(pushed_at) as last_push, latest(record_count) as records by bundle_id, entity_code
| join type=left entity_code [
    | inputlookup dora_group_entities.csv
    | fields entity_code
    | eval expected=1
  ]
| where isnull(expected)
| table bundle_id, entity_code, last_push
value: Indicators are only valuable when propagated. Measuring which legal entities received each threat feed revision supports defensible evidence that group-wide information sharing obligations were operationally executed.
implementation: (1) Maintain `dora_group_entities.csv` authoritative list; (2) alert when entity misses two consecutive bundles; (3) exclude non-EEA branches if policy dictates; (4) correlate with firewall rule update logs optionally; (5) quarterly attestation by CISO.
visualization: Table (missed entities), Heatmap entity x bundle, Single value (coverage %).
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
